intro: The Redmond Tech Orchestra (RTO) is a community orchestra based in Redmond, Washington. We are a nonprofit organization dedicated to fostering a vibrant cultural environment, supporting musical education, and ensuring that the joy of orchestral performances is accessible to all.

Founded by a couple of musicians working in tech, we are on a mission to provide opportunities for working adults to continue playing their instrument and sharing their love of music with the community.
We perform a wide variety of music, from classical to modern film scores. Come join RTO at one of our concerts throughout the year, and be amazed at our musicians' talent!

highlights.title: Community-Driven
highlights.body: Working professionals and lifelong musicians coming together to make music in the heart of the Eastside tech community.

highlights.title: Wide Repertoire
highlights.body: From Beethoven and Tchaikovsky to John Williams and Joe Hisaishi — programs that move audiences of all backgrounds.

highlights.title: Open to All
highlights.body: Concerts throughout the Puget Sound area, often free or low-cost, so the joy of orchestral music is accessible to everyone.

team.name: Peter Yang
team.bio: Peter is a conductor and arranger who also performs as a double bassist with multiple local ensembles. Since beginning to learn bass in 2011, he has played in orchestral, wind, and string settings. In high school, he was selected for district honor band and performed at Carnegie Hall with his school symphony orchestra. Today, he focuses on reimagining the orchestral experience for modern audiences by curating programs that pair classical repertoire with music people already know and love. His goal is to make the concert hall feel more accessible, engaging, and relevant to a broader community.

team.name: Jada Campbell
team.bio: Jada Campbell is an award-winning concert pianist whose performances have captivated audiences across the United States and Europe. She has appeared as a soloist with the Redmond Tech Orchestra, performed at Carnegie Hall's Weill Recital Hall, and taken part in international festivals in France and Italy. A graduate of the Peabody Conservatory, Jada is also a dedicated educator, having founded and taught at her own piano studio. Her playing is celebrated for its expressive depth, technical mastery, and vibrant musical storytelling.

team.name: Nandhini Lakuduva
team.bio: Nandhini is a data engineer at Microsoft by day and a passionate musician by... well, also by day! She has been playing the flute since middle school and recently fell in love with the piccolo, adding it to her musical repertoire. She loves being part of a huge group of talented musicians and leveraging her creativity as the orchestra's marketing head — where her TikTok obsession actually comes in handy. In her free time, she's usually getting inspiration from her pet budgies, who are basically the ultimate jamming buddies — they sing all day, every day, no sheet music required!

team.name: Ethan Wong
team.bio: Ethan has been a part of the Redmond Tech Orchestra since its early days, and making music with the group is always a highlight of his week. On stage, Ethan is a versatile musician performing on both trumpet and saxophone. Behind the scenes, he serves as RTO's Treasurer and actively supports recruiting efforts to grow the orchestra's presence within the Microsoft community. He holds a degree in Finance from the University of Oregon and currently works as a Finance Manager at Microsoft.

memberQuotes.name: Isabella de Leon
memberQuotes.quote: From performing K-pop to Gershwin, and venues ranging from grand concert halls to charming city parks, RTO offers a diverse and vibrant musical experience. RTO is perfect for anyone looking to revive an old hobby, find a chamber group, or make new friends!

memberQuotes.name: Sven Boemer
memberQuotes.quote: I joined RTO because they were looking for trumpet players, and I'm so glad I did! It has been a blast playing everything from Dvořák symphonies to epic Star Wars scores. The last concert included challenging selections from Wicked and West Side Story, with some awesome brass features. Rehearsals are always fun, with a positive, welcoming energy. If you're looking to make music with a great crowd, come join RTO (especially if you play trumpet)!

memberQuotes.name: Bryan Reich
memberQuotes.quote: The experience has been exceptional. The people involved are passionate and give an amazing amount of time and talent to make this group more than I thought was possible. The repertoire selections are challenging and fun, and it has been a joy having a first-row seat to experience some of the amazing hidden musical talents that happen to also be my coworkers.
